siteConfig.name: SOLTERRA
siteConfig.description: Solterra Cliff House is a 42-suite five-star retreat set into the botanical cliffs above the Amalfi coastline — glasshouse dining, a stone-carved spa, and rooms built around the view.
siteConfig.location: Path of the Gods, Praiano, Amalfi Coast, Italy

amenities.title: Salt-water Infinity Pool
amenities.desc: Cantilevered above the cove, heated year-round.

amenities.title: Basalt Thermal Spa
amenities.desc: A stone-carved circuit built into the cliff.

amenities.title: Private Cove Access
amenities.desc: Direct stairs to a members-only inlet.

rooms.name: Conservatory Garden Room
rooms.description: Ground-floor rooms opening onto the lemon terraces, with limewashed walls, brass fittings, and a private sitting nook framed by climbing jasmine.

rooms.name: Cliffside View Room
rooms.description: Set two floors above the water, each room is oriented for uninterrupted sunset views, with a stone soaking tub positioned at the window.

rooms.name: Belvedere Suite
rooms.description: A corner suite wrapped in glass on two sides, with a sunken lounge, wood-burning fireplace, and a wraparound balcony that catches both sunrise and sunset.

rooms.name: Grotto Villa
rooms.description: A stand-alone villa carved into the rock face with direct stair access to a private cove, its own infinity plunge pool, and a resident host on call.

venues.name: Il Vetro
venues.description: Our signature glasshouse restaurant, built into the rock with a retractable roof. Chef Elena Moretti's tasting menu changes with what the coast gives up each morning.

venues.name: La Terrazza
venues.description: Wood-fired focaccia, citrus from the garden, and the coast's best espresso, served on a terrace that catches the morning light.

venues.name: The Grotto Bar
venues.description: A cave-cellar bar stocked with amaro and rare vermouth, where the sommelier pours by candlelight.

courses.item: Sea urchin, brown butter, lemon leaf
Slow-roast lamb, charred onion, salsa verde
Limoncello semifreddo, basil, olive oil crumble

chef.name: Elena Moretti
chef.title: Executive Chef
chef.bio: Raised in a fishing family on this coastline, Elena trained in Lyon and Modena before returning home to build a menu around what her father still brings in each morning.

treatments.name: Stone Ritual
treatments.description: Warm basalt stones and marjoram oil, worked in long strokes to release the coast from your shoulders.

treatments.name: Citrus & Salt Polish
treatments.description: A whole-body exfoliation using salt from the bay and oil pressed from our own lemon terraces.

treatments.name: Grotto Facial
treatments.description: Cold-water algae and marine collagen, finished with a cryo-jade roller drawn from our plunge pool.

treatments.name: Wild Herb Massage
treatments.description: Rosemary and bay laurel, harvested that morning from the hillside behind the spa.

gallery.alt: Cliffside room with soaking tub facing the sea

testimonials.name: Margaux L.
testimonials.quote: Every window at Solterra is composed like a painting. The staff remembered our anniversary without being told twice.

testimonials.name: David & Priya K.
testimonials.quote: We've stayed at most of the well-known coastal properties. This is the first one that felt personal rather than performed.

testimonials.name: Julian M.
testimonials.quote: The Grotto Villa's plunge pool at sunrise is worth the trip alone. Booking again before we'd even checked out.

testimonials.name: Anke B.
testimonials.quote: Il Vetro's tasting menu was the best meal of our trip through Italy — and we ate very well that week.

faqs.q: What time is check-in and check-out?
faqs.a: Check-in is from 3:00 PM and check-out is by 11:00 AM. Early arrival and late departure can be arranged in advance, subject to availability.
We welcome guests of all ages in our Garden Rooms, Suites and Villas. Cliffside View Rooms are recommended for guests 12 and older, given the open-tub layout.

faqs.q: Do you offer airport or port transfers?
faqs.a: Yes — private car transfers from Naples International Airport and boat transfers from Salerno or Positano can be arranged by our concierge at the time of booking.
Standard rates are fully refundable up to 7 days before arrival. Non-refundable rates offer a reduced price and can be modified only in exceptional circumstances.
Garden Rooms and the main restaurant are step-free from the lower terrace entrance. Please contact our concierge ahead of arrival so we can prepare accordingly.
Well-behaved dogs under 15kg are welcome in Garden Rooms and Villas for an additional fee. Please let us know in advance.

about.storyHeading: Three generations on one rock face
storyParagraphs: In 1961, Salvatore Greco converted his family's fishing house on the Path of the Gods into six rooms for the first travelers making their way down the coast on foot. His daughter added the glasshouse restaurant in 1988. Her son, Marco, now runs the property with the same instruction his grandfather gave him: never let a room compete with the view.

Today Solterra holds 42 rooms, suites, and one villa, three restaurants, and a spa carved directly into the basalt — but the terraces, the citrus grove, and the original stone stairs to the water are unchanged.

sustainability.title: Solar-Assisted Power
sustainability.desc: Rooftop solar covers roughly 40% of the property's energy load.

sustainability.title: Spring-Fed Water
sustainability.desc: Our thermal pools and gardens draw from the same natural spring, filtered and recirculated.

sustainability.title: Terrace Farming
sustainability.desc: Citrus, herbs, and vegetables for Il Vetro are grown on our own hillside terraces.

sustainability.title: Zero Single-Use Plastic
sustainability.desc: Glass, ceramic, and linen have replaced disposable service ware property-wide since 2022.

awards.title: World's Leading Cliffside Retreat, World Travel Awards
